vulnerabilities.evidence: CSP header missing — XSS and data injection protections are not enforced by browser.

ai_analysis.explanation: The Content-Security-Policy header is absent, removing a critical browser-enforced defense against XSS and data injection attacks. Without CSP, browsers have no instruction set for restricting resource origins.
ai_analysis.impact: Increases attack surface for XSS exploitation. Allows loading of arbitrary external scripts, styles, and frames. Enables clickjacking and data exfiltration to attacker-controlled domains.
mitigation: Implement a strict CSP: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-{random}'
Start with report-only mode (Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only) to detect violations without breaking functionality
Use CSP nonces or hashes instead of 'unsafe-inline'
Define report-uri or report-to for violation monitoring

vulnerabilities.evidence: X-Frame-Options missing — page may be embedded in an iframe (clickjacking risk).

ai_analysis.explanation: The X-Frame-Options header is absent, allowing the page to be embedded in iframes on external domains. This enables clickjacking attacks where users are tricked into clicking hidden elements.
ai_analysis.impact: Clickjacking attacks enabling unauthorized actions, UI redressing, phishing via deceptive overlays, and potential CSRF bypass.
mitigation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY (or SAMEORIGIN if iframe embedding is needed internally)
Alternatively, use CSP frame-ancestors directive: frame-ancestors 'none'
Test iframe embedding from external origins is properly blocked

vulnerabilities.evidence: HSTS missing — connections may be downgraded from HTTPS to HTTP (SSL stripping).

ai_analysis.explanation: Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header is missing. Without HSTS, browsers may accept HTTP connections, making users vulnerable to SSL stripping attacks where an attacker can downgrade HTTPS to HTTP.
ai_analysis.impact: Susceptibility to man-in-the-middle attacks, SSL stripping, and cookie theft over unencrypted connections. Particularly dangerous on public Wi-Fi networks.
mitigation: Add: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload
Ensure all HTTP traffic is permanently redirected (301) to HTTPS
Submit domain to HSTS preload list at hstspreload.org
Set max-age to at least 1 year (31536000 seconds)

vulnerabilities.evidence: TCP port 53 (DNS) is open and accepting connections

ai_analysis.explanation: An unnecessary or unexpected port is open and accepting connections. Exposed services expand the attack surface, especially if running outdated software or default credentials.
ai_analysis.impact: Direct exploitation of service vulnerabilities, brute-force attacks on authentication, lateral movement if internal services are exposed, and information disclosure about the technology stack.
mitigation: Close all ports not required for the application to function
Apply firewall rules (iptables, AWS Security Groups, etc.) to restrict access
Ensure all exposed services run with updated, patched software
Disable default accounts and change default credentials immediately
Segment internal services (database, admin) from the public-facing network

vulnerabilities.evidence: X-XSS-Protection missing — legacy browser XSS filter not enforced.

ai_analysis.explanation: The application at http://example.com was found to be vulnerable to Missing Security Header. Insufficient input validation or missing security controls allow this attack vector.
ai_analysis.impact: Exploitation of this vulnerability could lead to data compromise, unauthorized access, or degraded security posture.
mitigation: Apply strict server-side input validation and output encoding
Review application code for unsanitized data handling
Implement security-focused code review and automated SAST scanning
Apply the principle of least privilege across all application layers

vulnerabilities.evidence: X-Content-Type-Options missing — MIME sniffing attacks possible.

ai_analysis.explanation: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header is missing. Without this header, browsers may perform MIME-type sniffing, executing files as a different content type than declared.
ai_analysis.impact: MIME confusion attacks allowing execution of uploaded files as HTML or JavaScript, enabling XSS via file uploads.
mitigation: Add: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff to all responses
Ensure correct Content-Type headers are set on all responses

vulnerabilities.evidence: Referrer-Policy missing — full URL may leak in Referer headers to third parties.

Apply the principle of least privilege across all application layers
